plan: Trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk exposure.
Increase QQQI for greater income and portfolio stability.
Trim SMH and CRWD to further reduce sector and single-name concentration.
Buy AAPL, a high-quality name with a strong scanner read, to diversify.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% monthly potential, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. However, it will likely offer slower growth compared to other modes.

plan: Trim SPY to comply with concentration limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove high-risk leveraged exposure.
Increase QQQI to maintain a balanced income component.
Buy AVGO and AAPL, both high-quality tech names with strong scanner reads, to enhance growth.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth potential, keeping a small cash buffer.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly potential, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding quality tech names. Expect moderate drawdowns, but still susceptible to market swings.

plan: Trim SPY and QQQI to meet mandate and shift focus to higher growth potential.
Sell SOXL and SPAL as they are below stop and do not meet aggressive entry criteria.
Buy LEU and GUSH, the highest-scoring scanner setups with strong risk/reward ratios.
This strategy leans into leverage and high-conviction setups, with a smaller cash buffer.
Strict stops are crucial to manage the amplified risk of this aggressive approach.
aggressive.read: This aggressive growth approach targets around 4% monthly potential, stretching for higher returns. It concentrates in top-scoring setups and leverages exposure, but comes with significantly higher drawdown risk. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, amplifying losses quickly if stops are not strictly adhered to.

plan: Trim SPY and QQQ to meet mandate and reduce broad index exposure.
Significantly increase QQQI to maximize monthly distributions.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta tech exposure.
Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate high-risk, high-volatility holdings.
This strategy prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income over growth.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% monthly potential, focusing on steady distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta single names, favoring income-generating ETFs. While aiming for low drawdown, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in market selloffs.
